summary: Implemented Server-Sent Events (SSE) as an alternative to WebSockets for real-time notifications. SSE works through Kubernetes HTTP ingress while WebSocket fails. Tested and verified end-to-end notification delivery.

resolved.issue: WebSocket connections fail through Kubernetes ingress
resolved.solution: Implemented SSE endpoint (/api/notifications/stream) which works over standard HTTP and is fully supported by ingress

new_features_implemented.description: Server-Sent Events endpoint that provides real-time notification delivery through HTTP
new_features_implemented.result: SUCCESS - Notifications delivered in real-time when events occur

new_features_implemented.description: Manages SSE connections per user_id with automatic cleanup
EventSource-based client with reconnection logic and 60s polling fallback
Green/yellow dot on notification bell shows real-time connection status

test_results.sse_keep_alive: PASSED - Ping messages sent every 30 seconds
test_results.frontend_toast_notifications: PASSED - Toast displayed when notification arrives
test_results.frontend_badge_update: PASSED - Unread count increments without page refresh

critical_code_review_comments: SSE is preferred over WebSocket for environments where ingress may not support WebSocket upgrade
The dual notification system (SSE + WebSocket) ensures notifications are delivered regardless of infrastructure
updated_files: /app/frontend/src/components/NotificationBell.jsx - Refactored to use SSE instead of WebSocket
seed_data_creation: Created test ticket TEST_SSE_Notification_* for SSE verification
context_for_next_testing_agent: Real-time notifications are now working via SSE (Server-Sent Events). The endpoint is GET /api/notifications/stream?token={jwt}. WebSocket endpoint still exists at /ws/{token} but may not work through ingress. Frontend NotificationBell.jsx uses SSE as primary with 60s polling fallback.
